area: CLI + entry points (cli/main.py, cli/models.py, cli/utils.py, cli/config.py, cli/stats_handler.py, cli/announcements.py, root main.py, README usage)
summary:
INVOCATION. Two entry paths. (1) Interactive CLI: console script `tradingagents = "cli.main:app"` in pyproject.toml [project.scripts] (also `python -m cli.main`; Docker ENTRYPOINT ["tradingagents"]). The Typer app (name="TradingAgents", add_completion=True) has a single command `analyze(checkpoint: bool = --checkpoint, clear_checkpoints: bool = --clear-checkpoints)`. `--clear-checkpoints` calls `clear_all_checkpoints(DEFAULT_CONFIG["data_cache_dir"])` (deletes ~/.tradingagents/cache/checkpoints/*.db) then proceeds. At import time cli/main.py runs `load_dotenv()` then `load_dotenv(".env.enterprise", override=False)`. (2) Programmatic: root main.py builds `config = DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()`, sets deep_think_llm/quick_think_llm = "gpt-5.4-mini", max_debate_rounds=1, data_vendors all "yfinance", then `ta = TradingAgentsGraph(debug=True, config=config)`; `_, decision = ta.propagate("NVDA", "2024-05-10")` returns (final_state, decision_string).

PARAMETERS â€” interactive questionnaire `get_user_selections()` (8 steps, in order):
1. Ticker: `typer.prompt("", default="SPY")` â€” NOTE: cli/main.py defines local `get_ticker`/`get_analysis_date` that SHADOW the questionary versions star-imported from cli/utils.py, so ticker is NOT normalized/uppercased in the CLI path (utils' `normalize_ticker_symbol` = strip().upper() is dead code here). Exchange suffixes supported (SPY, CNC.TO, 7203.T, 0700.HK).
2. Analysis date: `typer.prompt` default = today (%Y-%m-%d); loops until valid YYYY-MM-DD and not in the future.
3. Output language: `ask_output_language()` â€” select from English (default), Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Hindi, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Arabic, Russian, or "custom" (free text). Stored as config["output_language"]; internal debate stays English, reports/final decision are translated.
4. Analysts: `select_analysts()` questionary checkbox over AnalystType enum (cli/models.py): values "market", "social", "news", "fundamentals" (displays: Market Analyst, Social Media Analyst, News Analyst, Fundamentals Analyst); at least 1 required. Selection is then re-ordered to canonical ANALYST_ORDER = ["market", "social", "news", "fundamentals"].
5. Research depth: `select_research_depth()` â€” Shallow=1, Medium=3, Deep=5. Mapped to BOTH config["max_debate_rounds"] and config["max_risk_discuss_rounds"]. Round semantics (graph/conditional_logic.py): bull/bear debate ends when investment_debate_state["count"] >= 2*max_debate_rounds (Bull and Bear alternate, Bull first, then Research Manager); risk debate ends when risk_debate_state["count"] >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds (rotation Aggressive -> Conservative -> Neutral, then Portfolio Manager).
6. LLM provider: `select_llm_provider()` returns (provider_key, backend_url): openai=https://api.openai.com/v1, google=None, anthropic=https://api.anthropic.com/, xai=https://api.x.ai/v1, deepseek=https://api.deepseek.com, qwen=https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1, glm=https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/, openrouter=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1, azure=None, ollama=http://localhost:11434/v1.
7. Models: `select_shallow_thinking_agent(provider)` and `select_deep_thinking_agent(provider)` -> `_select_model(provider, "quick"|"deep")` from MODEL_OPTIONS catalog in tradingagents/llm_clients/model_catalog.py (e.g. anthropic quick: claude-sonnet-4-6/claude-haiku-4-5/claude-sonnet-4-5; anthropic deep: claude-opus-4-6/claude-opus-4-5/claude-sonnet-4-6/claude-sonnet-4-5). OpenRouter fetches live model list from GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (top 5 + custom); Azure prompts for free-text deployment name; a "custom" choice prompts for an arbitrary model ID.
8. Provider-specific thinking config (only for 3 providers): google -> `ask_gemini_thinking_config()` returns "high"|"minimal" -> config["google_thinking_level"]; openai -> `ask_openai_reasoning_effort()` "medium"|"high"|"low" -> config["openai_reasoning_effort"]; anthropic -> `ask_anthropic_effort()` "high"|"medium"|"low" -> config["anthropic_effort"].

RUN FLOW (`run_analysis(checkpoint: bool = False)`): copies DEFAULT_CONFIG and sets keys: max_debate_rounds, max_risk_discuss_rounds, quick_think_llm, deep_think_llm, backend_url, llm_provider, google_thinking_level, openai_reasoning_effort, anthropic_effort, output_language, checkpoint_enabled. Creates StatsCallbackHandler, then `TradingAgentsGraph(selected_analyst_keys, config=config, debug=True, callbacks=[stats_handler])` (full signature: `__init__(self, selected_analysts=["market","social","news","fundamentals"], debug=False, config=None, callbacks=None)`). Calls `message_buffer.init_for_analysis(selected_analyst_keys)`. Creates results dirs, then monkey-patches MessageBuffer methods with decorators that tee to disk. Builds initial state via `graph.propagator.create_initial_state(ticker, analysis_date)` (signature: `create_initial_state(company_name, trade_date, past_context="")` â€” CLI passes NO past_context) and `args = graph.propagator.get_graph_args(callbacks=[stats_handler])` which returns {"stream_mode": "values", "config": {"recursion_limit": 100 (config key max_recur_limit), "callbacks": [...]}}. Then streams `graph.graph.stream(init_agent_state, **args)` directly inside a Rich Live layout, appending every chunk to `trace`; `final_state = trace[-1]`; `decision = graph.process_signal(final_state["final_trade_decision"])` â€” process_signal is now DETERMINISTIC (SignalProcessor.process_signal -> parse_rating regex, returns one of Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell, default "Hold"; no LLM call) and the CLI actually DISCARDS this decision value (never printed or saved).

CRITICAL BEHAVIORAL GAP for reimplementers: the CLI bypasses `TradingAgentsGraph.propagate()`. Consequences: (a) memory-log past_context is never injected and `memory_log.store_decision` / `_resolve_pending_entries` never run in CLI mode (decision log only works via the programmatic main.py path); (b) `--checkpoint` sets config["checkpoint_enabled"]=True but the SqliteSaver recompile + thread_id injection happen only inside propagate(), so the flag is effectively inert in the CLI path (only --clear-checkpoints does anything); (c) the per-run JSON state log `_log_state` -> {results_dir}/{ticker}/TradingAgentsStrategy_logs/full_states_log_{date}.json is NOT written by CLI runs.

STREAM-CHUNK PROCESSING: per chunk, dedupes chunk["messages"] by message.id; classifies via `classify_message_type` (HumanMessage with content "Continue" -> "Control", HumanMessage -> "User", ToolMessage -> "Data", AIMessage -> "Agent", else "System"); extracts text via `extract_content_string` (handles str/dict/list content blocks); records tool_calls (name+args). `update_analyst_statuses` derives statuses from accumulated report sections: analyst with report = completed, first without = in_progress, rest pending; when all done sets Bull Researcher in_progress. Research team: chunk["investment_debate_state"] bull_history/bear_history update section "investment_plan" with headers "### Bull Researcher Analysis"/"### Bear Researcher Analysis"; judge_decision -> "### Research Manager Decision", research team completed, Trader in_progress. chunk["trader_investment_plan"] -> section "trader_investment_plan", Trader completed, Aggressive Analyst in_progress. chunk["risk_debate_state"] aggressive/conservative/neutral histories -> section "final_trade_decision" with "### {X} Analyst Analysis" headers; judge_decision -> "### Portfolio Manager Decision" and all risk agents + Portfolio Manager completed. The 7 report sections (MessageBuffer.REPORT_SECTIONS, section -> (gating analyst key, finalizing agent)): market_report->(market, Market Analyst), sentiment_report->(social, Social Analyst), news_report->(news, News Analyst), fundamentals_report->(fundamentals, Fundamentals Analyst), investment_plan->(None, Research Manager), trader_investment_plan->(None, Trader), final_trade_decision->(None, Portfolio Manager). Fixed (non-selectable) agents: Research Team [Bull Researcher, Bear Researcher, Research Manager], Trading Team [Trader], Risk Management [Aggressive Analyst, Neutral Analyst, Conservative Analyst], Portfolio Management [Portfolio Manager]. Display: Rich Live, 4 fps, header/progress-table/messages-table/current-report/footer; footer shows "Agents: x/y | LLM: n | Tools: n | Tokens: Xk up Yk down | Reports: x/7 | elapsed mm:ss" via StatsCallbackHandler (thread-safe counters: on_llm_start + on_chat_model_start increment llm_calls; on_tool_start increments tool_calls; on_llm_end reads generation.message.usage_metadata input_tokens/output_tokens).

ARTIFACTS. During run (decorator-based tee in run_analysis): base dir `{config["results_dir"]}/{ticker}/{analysis_date}/` where results_dir defaults to ~/.tradingagents/logs (env TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR; cache dir ~/.tradingagents/cache via TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR; memory log ~/.tradingagents/memory/trading_memory.md via TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH). Files: (1) `message_tool.log` â€” appended lines "HH:MM:SS [Type] content-with-newlines-flattened" and "HH:MM:SS [Tool Call] name(k=v, ...)"; (2) `reports/{section_name}.md` â€” each of the 7 sections rewritten (overwrite, utf-8) every time that section updates (so debate sections show latest snapshot). Post-run: prompt "Save report?" (default Y), then "Save path (press Enter for default)" defaulting to `{cwd}/reports/{ticker}_{YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS}`; `save_report_to_disk(final_state, ticker, save_path)` writes organized subfolders: 1_analysts/{market,sentiment,news,fundamentals}.md, 2_research/{bull,bear,manager}.md (from investment_debate_state.bull_history/bear_history/judge_decision), 3_trading/trader.md (trader_investment_plan), 4_risk/{aggressive,conservative,neutral}.md (risk_debate_state histories), 5_portfolio/decision.md (risk_debate_state.judge_decision), plus consolidated `complete_report.md` with header "# Trading Analysis Report: {ticker}\n\nGenerated: <timestamp>" and sections "## I. Analyst Team Reports" through "## V. Portfolio Manager Decision". Then prompt "Display full report on screen?" (default Y) -> `display_complete_report(final_state)` renders all sections as Rich Markdown panels. Final-state keys consumed: market_report, sentiment_report, news_report, fundamentals_report, investment_debate_state{bull_history, bear_history, history, current_response, judge_decision, count}, trader_investment_plan, risk_debate_state{aggressive_history, conservative_history, neutral_history, history, latest_speaker, judge_decision, count}, final_trade_decision, messages.

STARTUP EXTRAS: prints ASCII art from cli/static/welcome.txt plus workflow banner "I. Analyst Team -> II. Research Team -> III. Trader -> IV. Risk Management -> V. Portfolio Management"; fetches announcements from CLI_CONFIG["announcements_url"]="https://api.tauric.ai/v1/announcements" with announcements_timeout=1.0s, silently falling back to a static GitHub link (announcements_fallback); if response has require_attention=true, blocks on Enter via getpass.

key_components.role: Typer app + `analyze` command (--checkpoint, --clear-checkpoints); MessageBuffer (REPORT_SECTIONS/FIXED_AGENTS/status state machine); get_user_selections 8-step questionnaire; run_analysis stream loop; per-run log/report tee decorators; save_report_to_disk; display_complete_report; local get_ticker/get_analysis_date that shadow cli.utils versions

questionary prompt helpers: select_analysts, select_research_depth (1/3/5), select_llm_provider (10 providers + base URLs), _select_model/select_shallow_thinking_agent/select_deep_thinking_agent, select_openrouter_model (live API fetch), ask_output_language, ask_gemini_thinking_config, ask_openai_reasoning_effort, ask_anthropic_effort
StatsCallbackHandler(BaseCallbackHandler): counts llm_calls/tool_calls and input/output tokens from usage_metadata; feeds footer stats
fetch_announcements/display_announcements: HTTP GET with silent fallback, optional require_attention pause
Programmatic entry example: DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy(), TradingAgentsGraph(debug=True), ta.propagate("NVDA", "2024-05-10") -> (final_state, decision)

DEFAULT_CONFIG: results_dir/data_cache_dir/memory_log_path under ~/.tradingagents (env-overridable), llm_provider/deep_think_llm/quick_think_llm, backend_url=None, thinking keys, checkpoint_enabled, output_language, max_debate_rounds=1, max_risk_discuss_rounds=1, max_recur_limit=100, data_vendors (all yfinance default), tool_vendors

TradingAgentsGraph: builds quick/deep LLM clients via create_llm_client + provider kwargs, GraphSetup, Propagator, SignalProcessor; propagate() adds memory-log resolve/store, checkpointer recompile, and full_states_log JSON â€” all of which the CLI path bypasses
Propagator.create_initial_state(company_name, trade_date, past_context="") and get_graph_args -> {stream_mode: "values", config: {recursion_limit: 100, callbacks}}
Round-count gates: debate ends at count >= 2*max_debate_rounds; risk ends at count >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds; Bull-first and Aggressive->Conservative->Neutral orderings
SignalProcessor.process_signal -> parse_rating (deterministic, no LLM); returns Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell
RATINGS_5_TIER + parse_rating regex heuristic (looks for 'Rating: X' line, falls back to first rating word, default 'Hold')
MODEL_OPTIONS per provider per quick/deep mode used by the model-selection prompts; get_model_options(provider, mode)

get_checkpointer (per-ticker SqliteSaver at {cache}/checkpoints/{TICKER}.db), thread_id (sha256(ticker:date)[:16]), checkpoint_step, clear_checkpoint, clear_all_checkpoints (used by --clear-checkpoints)
[project.scripts] tradingagents = "cli.main:app"; packages include tradingagents* and cli*; cli package-data static/*
Usage docs: install, API-key env vars, `tradingagents` / `python -m cli.main`, Python usage, decision log + checkpoint resume sections (lines 101-249)

llm_touchpoints: The CLI layer itself makes NO direct LLM calls. It constructs TradingAgentsGraph, which creates exactly two LLM client instances via create_llm_client(provider, model, base_url, **kwargs): quick tier from config["quick_think_llm"] and deep tier from config["deep_think_llm"]; provider-specific kwargs forwarded from CLI answers: google -> thinking_level, openai -> reasoning_effort, anthropic -> effort; the CLI's StatsCallbackHandler list is passed into the LLM constructors as callbacks.

Quick-tier LLM is bound (in tradingagents/graph/setup.py) to: Market/Social/News/Fundamentals analysts (each with a ToolNode of data tools bound â€” market: get_stock_data+get_indicators; social: get_news; news: get_news+get_global_news+get_insider_transactions; fundamentals: get_fundamentals+get_balance_sheet+get_cashflow+get_income_statement), Bull Researcher, Bear Researcher, Trader, and Aggressive/Neutral/Conservative risk debators. Free-text/structured outputs are the concern of the agents subsystem, not the CLI.

Deep-tier LLM is bound to: Research Manager and Portfolio Manager (both structured-output agents per repo conventions; PM renders a typed PortfolioDecision to markdown containing a '**Rating**: X' header).

SignalProcessor.process_signal (called by CLI as graph.process_signal(final_state["final_trade_decision"])) is NOT an LLM call anymore â€” it is the deterministic parse_rating regex; the quick LLM arg is accepted for backwards compatibility only. The CLI also discards this return value.

Reflector.reflect_on_final_decision (quick LLM, one-paragraph reflection on realized returns) exists in TradingAgentsGraph but only fires inside propagate()/_resolve_pending_entries â€” never in the CLI streaming path.

Non-LLM network prompts in the CLI: GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (only when OpenRouter provider chosen) and GET https://api.tauric.ai/v1/announcements (startup banner, 1s timeout, silent fallback).
external_dependencies: LLM provider API key â€” REQUIRED for any run today, one of: OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, DASHSCOPE_API_KEY (Qwen), ZHIPU_API_KEY (GLM), OPENROUTER_API_KEY; or Azure OpenAI creds via .env.enterprise; or local Ollama at http://localhost:11434/v1 (no key). Loaded via load_dotenv() + load_dotenv(".env.enterprise", override=False) at cli/main.py import.

yfinance (network to Yahoo Finance) â€” REQUIRED default data vendor for all four data categories (core_stock_apis, technical_indicators, fundamental_data, news_data); no key needed. Also used by propagate-path return resolution (yf.Ticker(...).history vs SPY).
ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY â€” OPTIONAL; only if data_vendors/tool_vendors switched to "alpha_vantage".
https://api.tauric.ai/v1/announcements â€” OPTIONAL telemetry-ish startup banner; 1.0s timeout, silent fallback string on any failure.
https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models â€” OPTIONAL; only fetched when OpenRouter provider selected; falls back to manual model-ID entry.

Local filesystem: ~/.tradingagents/{logs,cache,memory} (TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR / TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR / TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH overrides) and {cwd}/reports for the post-run save. SQLite (langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite) for opt-in checkpoints â€” only engaged via the programmatic propagate() path.
port_notes: PRESERVE: (1) Run parameter set: ticker (free text, default SPY, keep exchange suffixes; consider adding the upper-case normalization the current CLI accidentally dropped), analysis date (YYYY-MM-DD, not future, default today), analyst subset of [market, social, news, fundamentals] normalized to that fixed order, research depth 1/3/5 driving max_debate_rounds AND max_risk_discuss_rounds with stop conditions count >= 2*depth (bull/bear) and count >= 3*depth (3 risk debators), output_language. (2) The 7 report section keys and their finalizing agents (market_report, sentiment_report, news_report, fundamentals_report, investment_plan, trader_investment_plan, final_trade_decision) â€” these are the artifact contract. (3) Artifact layout: live tee to {results_dir}/{ticker}/{date}/message_tool.log + reports/{section}.md, optional post-run export to {cwd}/reports/{ticker}_{timestamp}/ with 1_analysts..5_portfolio subfolders + complete_report.md, and the section header strings used inside investment_plan/final_trade_decision (### Bull Researcher Analysis, ### Bear Researcher Analysis, ### Research Manager Decision, ### {Aggressive|Conservative|Neutral} Analyst Analysis, ### Portfolio Manager Decision). (4) Deterministic parse_rating 5-tier extraction (Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell, default Hold) â€” pure regex, port as-is, no LLM needed. SIMPLIFY/REPLACE: questionnaire steps 6-8 (provider, backend_url, quick/deep model picks, thinking-effort knobs) collapse entirely under Claude Agent SDK subscription auth â€” replace the quick/deep two-tier model split with two Claude model choices (e.g. a fast model for analysts/researchers/trader/risk debators, a stronger model for Research Manager + Portfolio Manager) or a single model; drop create_llm_client/backend_url/model_catalog. Drop the announcements fetch and OpenRouter model listing. Replace LangChain StatsCallbackHandler with SDK message/usage hooks if you want the LLM/Tools/Tokens footer. The Rich Live dashboard is cosmetic; a simple streamed progress log suffices, but keep the agent-status ordering logic if you want parity. DECIDE: the current CLI bypasses propagate(), so memory-log context injection, decision persistence to ~/.tradingagents/memory/trading_memory.md, full_states_log JSON, and the --checkpoint SqliteSaver are all dead in interactive runs â€” a faithful port can skip them; a corrected port should route the CLI through the propagate-equivalent so past_context reaches the Portfolio Manager and decisions are logged. Data tools must stay on yfinance (keyless) to honor the no-paid-API constraint; keep alpha_vantage as optional vendor only.
